commitMessage:
Fixed bug 473101: [content assist] Completion proposals fail to render
the declaring type of array clone() methods

Change-Id: Ia0705943aeab188eb1d42e770c37a35c51146047
dslRule:
<!id: jdt.signature.stripsignaturetofqn.to.gettypeerasure>
<!description: Replace SignatureUtil.stripSignatureToFQN with Signature.getTypeErasure then Signature.toString for improved type erasure handling.
This transformation is based on a fix for a bug related to completion proposals for array clone() methods.
>
<!severity: HINT>
org.eclipse.jdt.core.SignatureUtil.stripSignatureToFQN(java.lang.String.valueOf($var))
=>
org.eclipse.jdt.core.Signature.toString(org.eclipse.jdt.core.Signature.getTypeErasure(java.lang.String.valueOf($var)))
;;
summary: Replaces a specific chain of SignatureUtil.stripSignatureToFQN(String.valueOf(...)) with Signature.toString(Signature.getTypeErasure(String.valueOf(...))) for improved handling of type signatures.

commitMessage:
Bug 474073 - Replace new Boolean with Boolean.valueOf

Using `new Boolean()` results in the creation of a new object on the
heap, when the flyweight `Boolean.TRUE` and `Boolean.FALSE` are available.
Java 1.4 added a `Boolean.valueOf()` which can be used in place of
`new Boolean()` but which will use the existing flyweight values instead.

Globally change `new Boolean(...)` to `Boolean.valueOf(...)` and replace
`new Boolean(...).booleanValue()` with `Boolean.parseBoolean(...)`

Bug: 474073
Change-Id: Iffa371b458424619b2787d0f995728b06619a3ed
Signed-off-by: Alex Blewitt <alex.blewitt@gmail.com>
summary: Replaces 'new Boolean(String).booleanValue()' with 'Boolean.parseBoolean(String)' for direct primitive boolean parsing.

commitMessage: fixed compile warnings from bug 478673
dslRule:
<!id: use-diamond-operator-collections>
<!description: Replace explicit type arguments in collection/map constructor calls with diamond operator if inferable (Java 7+).>
<!severity: HINT>
new $ConcreteType$<$TypeArg$>() :: subtypeOf($ConcreteType$, "java.util.Collection") || subtypeOf($ConcreteType$, "java.util.Map")
=> new $ConcreteType$()
;;
summary: Replace explicit type arguments in constructor calls for Collection/Map types with the diamond operator.
